text:
Content creator Ludwig has made it to this summer’sOlympics, where he’ll commentate at the chessboxing event, which is exactly what it sounds like.

Ludwig started streaming onTwitchback in 2018 and over the years managed to reach over three million followers, before leaving the platform tosign an exclusivity dealonYouTube, in late 2021.

Today he has over six million subscribers and his popularity has seen him co-found anesportsteam and commentate on Super Smash Bros. Melee tournaments and… chessboxing.

Ludwig’s experience has seen him recruited to take part in this summer’s Olympics in Paris, where he will become an official commentator for the sport ofchessboxing – which is exactly what it sounds like.

The content creator announced the news on Twitter, where he said:

‘Chessboxing is back. This event is not livestreamed but I’ll be uploading a video on my channel when I’m back home.’

Sign up to theGameCentral newsletterfor a unique take on the week in gaming, alongside the latest reviews and more. Delivered to your inbox every Saturday morning.

He also added an image showcasing the four contestants, and added that there will only be two fights during the event.

CHESSBOXING IS BACKTHIS EVENT IS NOT LIVE STREAMED BUT ILL BE UPLOADING A VIDEO ON MY CHANNEL WHEN IM BACK HOME âï¸ ð¥https://t.co/v6NCe1Y5yP

If you’re wondering what chessboxing is exactly – which is understandable – it is a hybrid sport that combines chess and boxing.

‘Two combatants play alternating rounds of blitz chess and boxing until one wins by checkmate or knockout,’say Wikipedia.

You can also win if your opponent spends too much time during the chess part, or by boxing decision if the chess match ends in a draw.

Ludwig hosted a chessboxing event last year, as seen in the video above, which peaked at 315,000 viewers and included streamers as the contestants.

The video shows how competitors play two minutes of chess before switching to boxing for a minute and a half, before going back to chess again.

The chessboxing contestants at the Olympic won’t be able to earn an official medal, however, as it’s only funded by the event as an exhibition, which explains why there are so few entrants.

title: YouTube star Ludwig will commentate the Olympics' most bizarre sport | Metro News
description: YouTuber Ludwig is at the Olympics in Paris and will commentate on the hybrid sport of chessboxing, which is as weird as it sounds.
